NVIDIA Beats Q4 Estimates, Raises Guidance on AI Demand

By Sarah Chen | February 26, 2025

NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) reported fourth-quarter results that crushed Wall Street expectations, with revenue growing 93% year-over-year to $22.1 billion. The chipmaker also raised its guidance above consensus expectations for the upcoming quarter.

Data-center revenue, which includes AI accelerator chips, grew to $18.4 billion. CEO Jensen Huang said the company expects strong demand to continue as hyperscalers and enterprises invest heavily in AI infrastructure.

Gross margin expanded to 76% from 73% in the prior quarter, driven by favorable product mix and pricing power in the AI chip market. The company's outlook remains strong with guidance above Street estimates.

Analysts at Morgan Stanley maintained their Overweight rating, noting that NVIDIA's competitive moat in AI training and inference continues to widen.
